reasoning: Based on real-world news from the 2025 F1 season up to November 9, Charles Leclerc holds a massive points lead over Lewis Hamilton. Hamilton was trailing by 30 points in July, by 46 points in September, and lost an additional 14 points to Leclerc at the Mexican Grand Prix in October, pushing the deficit to roughly 60 points. At the Brazilian Grand Prix on November 9, both drivers suffered a DNF and scored no points. There are only three standard races left in the season (Las Vegas, Qatar, Abu Dhabi) with ~86 maximum available points if a sprint race is included. Given that Hamilton would have to almost perfectly maximize his points while Leclerc would have to consistently fail to score in the remaining three races, the probability of Hamilton passing Leclerc in the standings is virtually zero. The prediction market valued the probability at 6% on October 30 before the Brazil GP wiped out another opportunity to close the gap. The chance is as close to mathematically impossible as standard racing allows.
